_comment: Sentence embedding configuration. See docs/config/embedding_dim_rationale.md for mathematical justification of dimension choices.

pretrained_fixed_dim._description: Embedding methods whose output dimension is fixed by the pre-trained model architecture and cannot be changed without retraining.

sbert.rationale: Trained on NLI + STS pairs with cosine similarity objective. 384-dim balances expressiveness and inference speed. Strong baseline for semantic retrieval tasks.

sbert_finetuned.rationale: Fine-tuning adapts the general SBERT model to the specific vocabulary of each dataset type (e.g. 'large red 1' patterns for Type-B). Hypothesis: will outperform frozen SBERT on in-domain retrieval.

bert_mean.rationale: Full BERT model; mean pooling aggregates all token representations. Higher dim (768) provides more expressive target space but harder for CNN to regress to.

bert_pooler.rationale: Pooler output is a dense projection of the [CLS] token, originally trained for sentence-level classification. Comparison with bert_mean isolates the effect of pooling strategy.

tinybert_mean.rationale: 4-layer distilled BERT. 312-dim is intermediate between TF-IDF (100) and BERT (768). Tests whether knowledge-distilled models retain enough semantic structure for this task.

tinybert_pooler.rationale: Compares pooler vs mean pooling for TinyBERT, mirroring the bert_mean vs bert_pooler comparison.

word2vec_pretrained.rationale: 3M-word Google News vocabulary. 300-dim vectors trained on large news corpus. Type-B numbers may be OOV; lowercase=True to match vocabulary. Mean pooling ignores word order (bag-of-words).

configurable_dim._description: Embedding methods where the output dimension is a free hyperparameter we choose. Choices are justified below.

word2vec_skipgram.rationale: Type-B vocabulary: 2 sizes + 4 colours + ~1251 number tokens = ~1257 unique tokens. With N=10,008 sentences, the effective corpus is small. Johnson-Lindenstrauss lemma gives minimum dim ~ O(log N) ~ 14; dim=100 provides 7x headroom while avoiding overfitting sparse co-occurrence statistics. dim>100 risks over-parameterisation given the limited corpus size.

tfidf.rationale: Type-B sentences use at most 3 tokens each ('large red 12'). Total unique tokens ~1257. TF-IDF with max_features=100 keeps the 100 highest-IDF tokens, covering all colour/size words plus the most distinctive numbers. Higher dim adds near-zero columns for low-IDF tokens, adding noise. No SVD compression applied since dim=100 < vocab_size, making sparse→dense straightforward.

tfidf_w2v.rationale: Dimension is set equal to word2vec_skipgram dim=100 to enable fair comparison between plain W2V mean-pool and TF-IDF-weighted W2V. The weighting re-weights tokens by their corpus-level discriminative power rather than uniform averaging.

glove.rationale: GloVe Wikipedia+Gigaword. Numeric tokens (e.g. '1234') are likely OOV. dim=100 is chosen to match word2vec_skipgram and tfidf for fair non-pretrained comparison, while being smaller than pretrained Word2Vec (300) to reflect the shallower Wikipedia coverage of number words.
